title: phacelia secunda subsp. pinnata
intro: Phacelia secunda subsp. pinnata is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its pinnate leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant qualities. The health benefits of Phacelia secunda subsp. pinnata include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Phacelia secunda subsp. pinnata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to support respiratory health and overall well-being.
